data.title: Gaxos.ai Signs At-The-Market Offering Agreement With H.C. Wainwright
data.description:
Gaxos.ai entered into an At-The-Market Offering Agreement to sell up to $3.0 million of common stock through H.C. Wainwright as sales manager. Shares may be sold from time to time on Nasdaq or other markets, with the company directing price, timing, and size. Gaxos.ai will pay a 3.0% commission on gross proceeds and can suspend or terminate the program at any time.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: At-The-Market Offering Agreement
  • Counterparty: H.C. Wainwright
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 23 2026 / Jan 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Raise capital flexibly through at-the-market sales

Original SEC Filing: GAXOS.AI INC. [ GXAI ] - 8-K - Jan. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: 374Water Files 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing >SCWO
data.description:
374Water Inc. (SCWO) filed a Form 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on January 23, 2026.

On January 21, 2026, Mr. Stephen J. Jones, a member of the Board of Directors (the "Board") of 374Water Inc. (the "Company"), notified the Board of his decision to resign from the Board. Mr. Jones' decision to resign was due to his desire to focus more attention on his role as Interim Chief Executive Officer and President (the "Interim CEO Role") of the Company, and the related strategic activities. In addition, Mr. Jones is involved in two other public company boards of directors and the Board role at the Company combined with the Interim CEO Role did not leave him sufficient time to fully undertake all his various obligations. Mr. Jones' resignation was not due to any disagreement with the Company on any matter relating to the Company's operations, policies or practices.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/933972/000165495426000569/scwo_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/933972/000165495426000569/0001654954-26-000569-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: KB HOME SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
KB HOME, a prominent homebuilding company, has released its 2025 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance over the past year. The report provides insights into the company's revenue, profitability, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive homebuilding industry.

Financial Highlights

KB HOME reported total revenues of $6,236.2 million for 2025, reflecting a 10% decrease from the previous year, primarily due to lower homebuilding revenues. Housing gross profits were $1,153.2 million, down 20% from the previous year, impacted by price reductions and higher relative land costs. Operating income decreased by 34% to $507.1 million, primarily due to lower housing gross profits. Net income for the year was $428.8 million, a 35% decrease from 2024, influenced by softer market conditions and strategic price reductions. The diluted earnings per share (EPS) was $6.15, reflecting lower net income, partly offset by the favorable impact of common stock repurchases.

Business Highlights

In 2025, KB HOME's homebuilding operations accounted for 99.6% of total revenues, with financial services contributing the remaining 0.4%. The company operates in nine states and 49 major markets, organized into four segments: West Coast, Southwest, Central, and Southeast. Notably, the company announced its reentry into the Atlanta, Georgia market in 2025.

KB HOME delivered 12,902 homes in 2025, a 9% decrease from the previous year, with an average selling price of $481,400. The company implemented a simplified sales strategy focusing on a straightforward, transparent base price with limited concessions, aimed at offering competitive value against resale home prices. Approximately 50% of homes delivered in 2025 were to first-time homebuyers, with first-time and first move-up homebuyers accounting for over 75% of annual deliveries.

The company operates through divisions with local management teams, focusing on land acquisition, community development, and product offerings tailored to local demand. KB HOME ended 2025 with 271 active communities, a 5% increase from the previous year, reflecting investments in land and land development. The number of homes in backlog at the end of 2025 was 3,128, a 29% decrease from the previous year, partly due to an 18% improvement in average build time.

Looking ahead, KB HOME plans to bring its mix of homes delivered closer to historical averages in 2026, with a focus on increasing the proportion of Built to Order sales and homes delivered.

Strategic Initiatives

In 2025, KB HOME implemented a simplified sales strategy to stimulate demand by providing a straightforward, transparent base price with limited concessions, aiming to offer competitive value against area resale home prices. This strategy was rolled out community-by-community starting mid-February 2025. Additionally, the company moderated its investments in land and land development in the second quarter of 2025 while increasing share repurchases, maintaining land investments to support current growth projections.

KB HOME repurchased approximately 9.4 million shares of its common stock at a total cost of $538.5 million in 2025, compared to 4.7 million shares at a total cost of $350.0 million in 2024. The company also declared four quarterly cash dividends of $0.25 per share in 2025, up from $0.20 per share in the first quarter of 2024. On November 12, 2025, the company obtained an upsized $1.20 billion five-year Credit Facility, replacing the prior $1.09 billion unsecured revolving credit facility, and extended the maturity of its $360.0 million Term Loan to 2029. The company ended 2025 with total liquidity of $1.43 billion, including $228.6 million in cash and cash equivalents and nearly $1.20 billion of available capacity under the Credit Facility.

Looking forward, KB HOME plans to continue its disciplined and balanced approach in capital allocation, guided by market conditions and priorities of investing in land and land development to support future growth and returning capital to stockholders. In 2026, the company intends to continue investing in and developing land positions within attractive submarkets and selectively acquire or control additional land that meets its investment standards. The company expects to use or redeploy cash resources or cash borrowings under the Credit Facility to support its business within the context of prevailing market conditions.

Challenges and Risks

KB HOME operates in a highly competitive homebuilding industry, facing challenges from other homebuilders, resale homes, and rental housing. The company is focused on expanding within its current geographic footprint to achieve a top-five position in each served market. However, the business is concentrated in California, Florida, Nevada, and Texas, making it vulnerable to adverse conditions in these markets, particularly California, which has a highly regulated and litigious business environment.

Consumer demand risks include soft or negative economic conditions, elevated mortgage interest rates, and inflation, which could negatively impact consumer demand for homes. Reduced employment levels and unfavorable demographic changes could also affect demand, particularly among first-time and first move-up homebuyers. Housing affordability challenges, diminished consumer confidence, and tightened mortgage loan availability are additional risks.

Supply risks include a lack of available land, delayed community openings, and supply chain issues, including cost pressures and constrained availability of construction materials. These factors could negatively impact the company's ability to increase its lot inventory and community count, affecting operational scale and market share.

Regulatory risks involve extensive legal and regulatory requirements, which can delay operations and increase costs. Changes in tax laws, such as the repeal of the Section 45L tax credit, will impact future income tax expenses. Climate-related regulations, particularly in California, could increase construction costs and delay home construction.

Climate risk includes potential risks from global climate change, such as extreme weather events and regulatory developments. These could disrupt operations, increase costs, and affect profitability. California's new energy efficiency standards and solar power system requirements are specific challenges.

Operational risks involve reliance on independent contractors for land development and home construction, facing risks from poor contractor availability and performance. Supply chain disruptions and inflation have increased input costs, and the company may not be able to pass these costs onto consumers.

Information technology and information security risks include cybersecurity threats, such as phishing and denial-of-service attacks. The company has implemented measures to mitigate these risks but acknowledges that its IT resources and those of its service providers may be vulnerable.

Management is focused on enhancing asset efficiency by calibrating home sales rates and controlling construction costs. The company aims to increase inventory turns and optimize production to improve profitability. Management is also addressing supply chain challenges by expanding the supplier base and implementing cost-saving measures.

Market risks include exposure to interest rate fluctuations, which could affect mortgage loan availability and consumer demand. Inflationary pressures and supply chain disruptions are expected to continue, impacting costs and margins.

SEC Filing: KB HOME [ KBH ] - 10-K - Jan. 23, 2026

data.title: Soligenix Enters Sales Agreement With Rodman
Top News Today/Canada: Team Trump Livid After Carney Calls Out U.S. Coercion
data.description:
HEADLINES

Team Trump Is Livid After Canada's Carney Calls Out U.S. Coercion

Canada and the Trump administration are locked in a war of words over Prime Minister Mark Carney's bid to chart a new model for smaller powers to fight back against the U.S.'s aggressive use of its economic and military might.

In the past week, Carney has resolved a trade dispute with America's biggest strategic competitor, China, and delivered a speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland urging smaller powers to unite against economic coercion from the world's great powers.

The moves drew cheers at home and abroad as a template on how to stand up to the Trump administration's perceived bullying, just as the president was threatening Europe with retribution if Denmark didn't yield the Arctic island of Greenland to the U.S.

Retail Sales Pull Back After Rise in November

Canadians remain cautious with their spending, with retailers registering a pullback in sales for the final month 2025 that rounds out a year of choppy trade.

Retail sales in November increased 1.3% from the month, only for sales to drop an estimated 0.5% in December, based on an advance tally of receipts by the national data agency.

November's increase, while the strongest in five months and modestly sharper than the 1.2% rise in sales that economists had expected, followed weakened sales the previous two months, including a 0.3% slide in October.

Bausch Health Liver Disease Trials Miss Primary Endpoint

Bausch Health's two phase three trials evaluating a treatment for adults with liver cirrhosis to prevent hepatic encephalopathy, a complication of liver disease, missed their primary endpoint.

Shares dropped 11% to C$8.07

"We are disappointed in the results, as there is currently no approved treatment for these patients. We are currently reviewing the full dataset to determine potential new development opportunities," said Chief Executive Thomas Appio.

The trials were both safe and well-tolerated.

Factory Sales Rose an Estimated 0.5% in December

Factory activity in Canada recovered somewhat to close out last year, with an early estimate pointing to a lift in sales thanks to the food subsector and a recovery in the motor vehicle industry.

Manufacturing shipments rose an estimated 0.5% in December from the month before, Statistics Canada said.

That would mark the second rise in five months, and partly reverses a 1.2% drop in sales in November when global semiconductor shortages weighed heavily on the auto industry. In volume terms, factory sales were down an even sharper 2.3% in November.

Bombardier's Global 8000 Jet Receives European Union Aviation Safety Agency Certification

Bombardier's Global 8000 has received certification from the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.

The plane maker said that its ultra-long-range aircraft entered into service in December, after receiving certifications from U.S. and Canadian aviation authorities. The Global 8000 is the fastest civilian aircraft since the Concorde, and offers the lowest cabin altitude in business aviation, the company said.

The lower cabin altitude may minimize the physiological stress of high altitude travel, and produce a more comfortable flight experience for passengers, it added.

Travel to U.S. Remains Down Sharply

Canadians continue to shun travel to the U.S.

Return trips by Canadians to the nearest neighboring country slumped 23.6% on-year in November, though Americans similarly look to be avoiding Canada with inbound journeys by U.S. residents down 9%.

Data from Statistics Canada shows that overall, Canadians returned from 3.3 million trips abroad in November, down 14.4% on-year. That included a 28.2% drop in return trips from the U.S. by automobile and a 12.1% fall in trips by air from the U.S.

In contrast, Canadian-resident return trips by air from overseas countries were up 14.1%. Also in November, 324,200 overseas residents arrived in Canada, up 13.6%, with Europe and Asia the top two source markets.

TALKING POINT

Why BYD's First Manufacturing Foothold in Canada Was a Bust

By Jason Kirby for The Globe and Mail

With Canada opening the door slightly to Chinese electric passenger vehicles, Shenzhen-based carmaker BYD has been cited as one company that could benefit and be a potential investor in the sector.

But the Chinese auto giant has already made one foray into vehicle manufacturing in Canada that ended poorly: an electric bus assembly plant in Newmarket, Ont., that closed its doors after a handful of years, a victim of quality issues and political tensions between Ottawa and Beijing.

In 2019, BYD, now the world's largest EV maker, trumpeted the opening of a 45,000-square-foot bus assembly plant in Newmarket, with a focus on producing an initial trial run of 10 electric buses for the Toronto Transit Commission — which would ultimately be the sum total of the plant's output.

The Ontario plant was BYD's second in North America after a bus assembly facility in Lancaster, Calif., and the first bus rolled out the door the following year.

Today the company's name is stripped from the building, which has since been sold, marking the end of what the company saw as a foothold for its expansion into Canada.

Even in the U.S., where the company's California plant produces electric school buses, the BYD name, which stands for "build your dreams," is gone. In 2022, the company's North American operations rebranded as RIDE, an acronym for "real innovation delivered with excellence."

The company retains a "handful" of employees in Canada, said Frank Girardot, RIDE's senior communications director. After the Newmarket facility completed the project for the TTC, it ceased operations, he said, declining to elaborate on the closing, or the status of any outstanding contracts.

What's clear is that from early on, there were signs of quality issues with BYD's buses in Canada.

"A big problem the TTC had when they were running the BYD buses was getting parts," said Steve Munro, a Toronto transit watcher and blogger. "BYD didn't have the whole supply chain that you'd expect anybody in the automotive industry would have to service people who own their product."

The TTC documented the challenges posed by the vehicles in a 2022 report, which stated it was extremely difficult to obtain replacement parts. According to the report, it took BYD 73 days to source a replacement rear axle for one bus.

"BYD has not provided sufficient engineering support on site and remotely," the report stated. "Repair manuals and procedures are of poor quality and BYD's diagnostic tool is currently not available in English."

Other municipalities that acquired BYD buses faced similar problems.

In 2017, St. Albert, Alta., became the first municipality in Canada to add long-range electric buses to its transit fleet, eventually buying a total of seven BYD buses by the following year. The vehicles were built at the California plant.

However, in 2023, the city revealed three of the seven vehicles required full battery replacements within the first five years of operation, while all seven required replacement of propulsion components like drive motors and gearboxes, according to the St. Albert Gazette. The battery replacements each took 80 days to complete.

Meanwhile, Montreal's transit authority bought four buses from BYD in 2018 and 2019 that were never put into use because the battery range proved to be insufficient and the buses were not suitable for customers with reduced mobility, according to Amélie Régis, a spokesperson for Société de transport de Montréal. The buses are now used for day camps and corporate contracts.

Ted Dowling, the Canadian executive who served as vice-president of BYD Canada at the time, but who left the company in 2022, acknowledges that "at the start the buses had some issues, but if you talk to the TTC and other operators they're very happy with them."

In an email, TTC spokesperson Stuart Green said because the BYD buses have a charging system that is incompatible with its infrastructure they are all in storage, while the transit agency tries to find new homes for them.

Rising tensions between Canada and China compounded BYD's struggles in Canada, Dowling said, citing Beijing's detention of Michael Spavor and Michael Kovrig.

The pandemic also presented challenges, he said, though with the TTC order completed, BYD shifted gears at the facility in 2020 and used it to instead make and store personal protective equipment for the Ontario and federal governments.

A 2021 report from Ontario's auditor-general showed that as of Dec. 31, 2020, BYD Canada had received $24.6 million for PPE gear.

Once Canada implemented a 100% tariff on Chinese electric vehicles in 2024 and launched consultations about further duties, "that shut everything down" in terms of BYD's interest in Canada, said Dowling, though by then he'd left the company and the plant had closed.

Now Canada's restrictions on Chinese EVs are lifting, with the federal government planning to allow 49,000 Chinese EVs to enter the market each year at a reduced tariff rate of 6.1%. Part of the quota will be reserved for vehicles that cost less than $35,000.

The beneficiaries of that, at least for the portion above $35,000, will likely be Tesla and Polestar, said Dowling. "If you think a big company like BYD is going to spend $10 million" to adapt its vehicles for sale in Canada "for a market share of maybe 10,000 cars a year, it's not going to happen."

That said, BYD's Chinese rival Chery Automobile Co. Ltd. is positioning itself to sell its EVs in Canada.

There is the possibility BYD may eventually invest in auto production in Canada, but doing so would require BYD to build extensive capacity to service its vehicles, he said.

data.title: Intel Returns to Losses as Supply Shortages, Spending Weigh on Q4 Results and Forecast — WSJ
data.description:
By Dan Gallagher and Sean McLain

Intel's latest earnings report made clear that the chip-making company's turnaround is still a work in progress. The stock — which has more than doubled in value over the past six months — slid 18% in Friday trade.

The company lost money in the fourth quarter and is forecasting more losses in the first quarter of this year, as it spends heavily to ramp up production of its latest chips while contending with an inventory shortage.

The shortage makes it more challenging for Intel to capitalize on a surge in demand for data-center servers that use its chips. Cloud-computing giants that have been investing significantly in artificial-intelligence infrastructure are also now upgrading some of their older server lines.

Intel has depleted its inventory of those products and is pushing to make more, executives said on the company's fourth-quarter earnings call Thursday. They admitted to misjudging demand, with Chief Financial Officer David Zinser saying "we directionally weren't managing the supply to an expectation that there would be a unit increase that significantly" in the data-center business.

Intel's shortfall could create more opportunity for Advanced Micro Devices, its main rival in these types of server chips. AMD's stock jumped nearly 4% on Friday morning.

"I'm disappointed that we are not able to fully meet the demand in our markets," Intel Chief Executive Lip-Bu Tan said on Intel's call. "We are on a multiyear journey. It will take time and resolve."

Expectations for a major boost in data-center sales had propelled Intel's stock price up more than 130% over the past six months. That run-up put Intel's stock price to a multiple of about 88 times the company's projected earnings for this year-- expensive for any chip stock . Friday's drop puts Intel on pace for its worst selloff in nearly 18 months.

Another major factor driving the stock of late has been hopes for the company's burgeoning foundry business, where Intel is seeking to become a contract manufacturer for chips designed by other companies. Intel needs a major customer for that business to make it economically viable. Its foundry segment had more than $10 billion in operating losses in 2025.

Rumors have been swirling in recent weeks that Intel already landed at least one major customer. But the company made clear Thursday that any client announcement is still a ways off.

"We believe customers will begin to make firm supplier decisions starting in the second half of this year and extending into the first half of 2027," Tan said.

Intel reported a net loss of $333 million during the last three months of the year, worse than the $294 million loss expected by analysts, according to FactSet.

Revenue for the period was $13.7 billion, compared with $14.3 billion a year prior. Sales for its data-center business rose 9% year over year to $4.7 billion in the fourth quarter.

Intel projected a loss per share of 21 cents for the first quarter of 2026 and revenue between $11.7 billion and $12.7 billion.

Even with the lack of news about a major foundry customer, Intel made clear that it was sticking to its previously stated plan of not putting major capital investments into its next manufacturing process called 14A until it has landed enough customers to make it viable.

Intel has lagged behind rivals Nvidia and AMD in the race to develop chips to power the boom in AI data centers. Its foundry business has fallen far behind those of chip-making giants Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. and Samsung Electronics.

But an infusion of cash from investors, including the U.S. government, and a deal to develop chips with Nvidia, has helped push up Intel shares. In August, when SoftBank injected $2 billion in the beleaguered chip maker, Intel shares were trading at around $23. Since then, they have more than doubled in price to $54.

The first computers carrying chips based on Intel's new 18A manufacturing process went on sale in January. Investors say demand for the chips, dubbed Panther Lake, are an important indicator of whether Intel's turnaround plans are working.

"Intel is probably not out of the woods in terms of competitiveness right now. You're going to need Panther Lake to get back on the right track," said David Wagner, the head of equity at Aptus Capital Advisors. "It comes down to customers and their adoption."

Write to Dan Gallagher at dan.gallagher@wsj.com and Sean McLain at sean.mclain@wsj.com

Watch: The U.S. Took a Stake in Intel Last Year

data.title: Achieve Life Sciences May Offer Up To $300 Million In Securities
data.description:

data.title: Affinity Bancshares Q4 net income rises 62% yr/yr
data.description:
Overview

  • Company's Q4 net income rises to $2.1 mln from $1.3 mln yr/yr

  • Company's net interest income for Q4 increased to $8.3 mln

  • Q4 net interest margin improved to 3.77% from 3.56% yr/yr

Outlook

  • Affinity Bancshares did not provide specific guidance for future financial performance

Result Drivers

  • NET INTEREST INCOME - Increase in net interest income attributed to higher interest income on loans and deposits, despite higher deposit costs

  • NONINTEREST EXPENSES - Decrease in noninterest expenses due to lower salaries and employee benefits

  • NET INTEREST MARGIN - Improved net interest margin due to decreased yields on interest-bearing liabilities, offset by lower yields on interest-earning deposits and investments

data.title: KS Bancorp Q4 net income surges on loan demand
data.description:
Overview

  • North Carolina bank's Q4 net income rose 55% yr/yr, driven by loan demand

  • Company's total assets grew to $822.6 mln, reflecting strong loan and deposit growth

  • Company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.30 per share

Outlook

  • Company remains focused on disciplined balance sheet management and strategic investments

Result Drivers

  • LOAN DEMAND - Continued loan demand across the branch network drove net loan balances up by $109.7 mln to $664.2 mln

  • NET INTEREST INCOME - Net interest income before credit losses rose 24.3% to $7.7 mln, supporting earnings growth

  • DEPOSIT GROWTH - Total deposits increased by $131.4 mln, including a $35.3 mln rise in core deposits

data.title: Silver Elephant Closes First Tranche of Non-Brokered Private Placement for Gross Proceeds of $565,000
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - January 23, 2026) - Silver Elephant Mining Corp.  (OTCQB: SILEF) (FSE:1P2) ("Silver Elephant" or the "Company") announces that, further to its news release dated January 19, 2026, it has closed the first tranche of its non-brokered private placement (the "Private Placement") raising gross proceeds of $565,000 through the sale of 2,260,000 units (the "Units") at a price of $0.25 per unit. Each Unit consists of one common share of the Company (each, a "Share") and one share purchase warrant (each, a "Warrant") with each warrant entitling the holder to purchase one additional Share at a price of $0.32 per Share for a period of three years from issuance.

In connection with the Private Placement, the Company issued 21,000 finder's units (the "Finder's Units") to eligible finders. The finder's warrants underlying the Finder's Units have the same terms and conditions as the Warrants comprising the Units.

A 10% insider of the Company and an officer of the Company (collectively, the "Insiders") participated in the Private Placement for an aggregate total of 210,000 Units, which participation constitutes a "related party transaction" within the meaning of Multilateral Instrument 61-101 - Protection of Minority Security Holders in Special Transactions ("MI 61-101"). The Company relied on exemptions from the formal valuation and minority shareholder approval requirements provided under sections 5.5(a) and 5.7(a) of MI 61-101 on the basis that the Insiders participation in the Private Placement did not exceed 25% of the fair market value of the Company's market capitalization. The Company will file a material change report in respect of the related party transaction.

The securities issued under the Private Placement will be subject to a regulatory hold period of four months plus one day from the date of issue. Proceeds of the Private Placement are expected to be used for general corporate purposes. The second and final tranche of the Private Placement is expected, subject to regulatory approval, to close on or around January 27, 2026.

This news release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy nor shall there be any sale of the Units in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation, or sale would be unlawful prior to the registration or qualification under the securities laws of that jurisdiction.

About Silver Elephant Mining Corp.

Silver Elephant is a mineral exploration company focused on the development of its silver-lead-zinc project in Bolivia

Further information on Silver Elephant can be found at www.silverelef.com.

SILVER ELEPHANT MINING CORP.

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD

"John Lee"

CEO and Executive Chairman

For more information about Silver Elephant, please contact Investor Relations:
